chapter: Chapter 3: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables

questions.question: Justify that a pair of linear equations cannot have exactly two solutions.
questions.answer: Two straight lines can intersect at only one point, be parallel, or coincide. Hence, a pair of linear equations can have one, zero, or infinitely many solutions, but never exactly two.

questions.question: Explain why determinant equal to zero does not always imply no solution.
questions.answer: When determinant is zero, the equations may represent coincident lines, resulting in infinitely many solutions.

questions.question: Explain why slope plays a key role in determining consistency.
questions.answer: Slope determines whether lines intersect, are parallel, or coincide, directly affecting consistency.

questions.question: Explain why parallel lines never represent a solution.
questions.answer: Parallel lines have equal slopes and never intersect, so no common solution exists.

questions.question: Explain why coincident lines represent infinitely many solutions.
questions.answer: Every point on one line lies on the other, so infinite solutions exist.

questions.question: Explain why algebraic methods are more reliable than graphical methods.
questions.answer: Algebraic methods provide exact solutions and are independent of drawing accuracy.

questions.question: Explain why real-life problems usually require algebraic solutions.
questions.answer: Real-life problems require precise values, which algebraic methods provide.

questions.question: Explain the failure of cross-multiplication when equations are dependent.
questions.answer: In dependent equations, determinant becomes zero, so cross-multiplication fails.

questions.question: Explain why graphical interpretation is important despite its limitations.
questions.answer: Graphs help visualize relationships and nature of solutions even if exact values are not obtained.

questions.question: Explain how slope-intercept form helps in understanding solutions.
questions.answer: Slope-intercept form clearly shows slope and intercept, helping compare lines easily.

questions.question: Explain why linear equations cannot model exponential growth.
questions.answer: Because linear equations show constant rate of change, while exponential growth increases multiplicatively.

questions.question: Explain why a unique solution is called an independent solution.
questions.answer: Because the equations are independent of each other and intersect at one point.

questions.question: Explain why infinitely many solutions indicate dependency.
questions.answer: Because one equation can be derived from the other, making them dependent.

questions.question: Explain why determinant decides the solvability of equations.
questions.answer: Determinant indicates whether equations intersect uniquely, coincide, or are parallel.

questions.question: Explain why exact solutions are necessary in engineering problems.
questions.answer: Engineering problems require accuracy to ensure safety and efficiency.

questions.question: Explain how pair of linear equations form the foundation for higher mathematics.
questions.answer: They introduce systems, functions, matrices, and linear algebra concepts.

questions.question: Explain why every solution of a dependent system satisfies both equations.
questions.answer: Because both equations represent the same line.

questions.question: Explain why graphical errors increase with improper scaling.
questions.answer: Improper scaling distorts graph accuracy, leading to incorrect intersection points.

questions.question: Explain how linear equations help in decision making.
questions.answer: They help compare alternatives and optimize resources.

questions.question: Explain the limitations of linear equations in modelling.
questions.answer: They cannot represent non-linear or exponential relationships.

questions.question: Explain why intersecting lines guarantee a unique solution.
questions.answer: Because they meet at only one point.

questions.question: Explain why pair of linear equations are called simultaneous equations.
questions.answer: Because they are solved together to satisfy both equations at the same time.

questions.question: Explain the importance of slope comparison in graphical solutions.
questions.answer: Slope comparison helps identify whether lines intersect, are parallel, or coincide.

questions.question: Explain how linear equations are applied in economics.
questions.answer: They are used to model cost, revenue, profit and demand relationships.

questions.question: Explain the overall importance of pair of linear equations in mathematics.
questions.answer: They form the basis for algebra, systems of equations and real-life problem solving.
